offset-pagination.label: OFFSET and Pagination
offset-pagination.summary: Skip rows and paginate results with OFFSET.

sketch2.title: Why ORDER BY Matters with OFFSET

offset-intro.title: What is OFFSET?
offset-intro.bodyMarkdown:
When you want to skip a certain number of rows in your SQL query results, you can use the `OFFSET` clause. This is especially useful for pagination—showing results page by page. For example, if you want to display results 6–10, you can skip the first 5 rows using `OFFSET 5`.

Example:
```sql
SELECT * FROM products
ORDER BY id
LIMIT 5 OFFSET 5;
```
This query returns 5 rows, starting after the first 5 rows in the `products` table.

offset-pagination.title: Paginating with LIMIT and OFFSET
offset-pagination.bodyMarkdown:
Pagination is the process of dividing results into pages. In SQL, you can combine `LIMIT` and `OFFSET` to fetch a specific 'page' of results. For example, to get the second page of 2 products each, use:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products
ORDER BY id
LIMIT 2 OFFSET 2;
```
This skips the first 2 products and returns the next 2.

offset-ordering.title: Why ORDER BY Matters with OFFSET
offset-ordering.bodyMarkdown: Always use `ORDER BY` when using `OFFSET` to ensure consistent and predictable results. Without `ORDER BY`, the rows skipped or returned might change each time you run the query, since SQL tables do not guarantee row order by default.

offset-basic-single-choice.title: Purpose of OFFSET
offset-basic-single-choice.prompt: What is the main purpose of the OFFSET clause in a SQL query?

help.concept: OFFSET is used to skip a certain number of rows before starting to return rows from a query result.
help.hint_1: Think about how you would display the second page of results.
help.hint_2: OFFSET tells SQL how many rows to skip before starting to return results.

options.a: To limit the number of columns returned
options.b: To skip a specified number of rows before returning results
options.c: To remove duplicate rows from the results
options.d: To sort the results in descending order

offset-pagination-code-input.title: Paginate Products Table
offset-pagination-code-input.prompt: Write a SQL query to return the 3rd and 4th products (by id) from the products table.
offset-pagination-code-input.hint: Use ORDER BY, LIMIT, and OFFSET to get the right rows.

help.concept: To paginate, combine ORDER BY (for consistent order), LIMIT (for how many rows), and OFFSET (for how many to skip).
help.hint_1: You want to skip the first two products and get the next two.
help.hint_2: Use LIMIT 2 OFFSET 2 after ordering by id.

offset-drag-reorder.title: Paginate with OFFSET: Steps
offset-drag-reorder.prompt: Arrange the steps to paginate results using OFFSET and LIMIT.
offset-drag-reorder.hint: Think about the logical order of SQL clauses.

help.concept: SQL queries are built step by step: select columns, choose table, order rows, then limit and offset results.
help.hint_1: ORDER BY comes before LIMIT and OFFSET.
help.hint_2: LIMIT and OFFSET are usually at the end, after ordering.

tokens.t2: Add FROM clause with table name
tokens.t3: Add ORDER BY to define row order
tokens.t4: Add LIMIT to specify number of rows to return

offset-fill-blank-choice.title: Fill in the OFFSET Value
offset-fill-blank-choice.prompt: Suppose you want to display the third page of results, with 2 products per page. What OFFSET value should you use?
offset-fill-blank-choice.hint: Each page skips a certain number of rows.

help.concept: OFFSET is calculated as (page number - 1) × page size.
help.hint_1: For the third page, how many rows are before it if each page has 2 rows?
help.hint_2: Multiply 2 (rows per page) by 2 (pages before the third).

offset-fill-blank-choice.template: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY id LIMIT 2 OFFSET [___];

offset-multi-choice.title: Best Practices with OFFSET
offset-multi-choice.prompt: Which of the following are good practices when using OFFSET in SQL? (Select all that apply.)
offset-multi-choice.hint: Think about what OFFSET does and how to get predictable results.

help.concept: ORDER BY ensures consistent row order with OFFSET. OFFSET can be used alone, but LIMIT is often used for pagination.
help.hint_1: OFFSET skips rows, not columns, and needs ORDER BY for consistency.
help.hint_2: Using OFFSET without ORDER BY can lead to unpredictable results.

options.a: Always use ORDER BY with OFFSET
options.b: Use OFFSET without LIMIT to get all remaining rows after skipping
options.d: OFFSET guarantees the same rows every time, even without ORDER BY
